data.narrative:
Dhruva Space is a Series B-stage space and aerospace company. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 3 same-sector, same-stage private companies (Aalyria, Hubble Network, and Sift) alongside a broader universe of 5 public Space & Aerospace comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 additional private peers for context.

The peer-median method is the primary applicable triangulation approach. The median valuation across the three private comparables is $400M, with valuations ranging from $274M (Sift) to $1.3B (Aalyria). Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples cannot be applied, as Dhruva Space's revenue is not publicly disclosed; the Space & Aerospace sector median P/S stands at 53.2x across 6 public tickers, but without revenue data this multiple cannot be meaningfully applied to the target.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $340M to $460M, centered on $400M. This range reflects the peer-median benchmark and near-peer positioning within the $274M–$1.3B spread observed in the comparable set. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion among same-stage peers and the absence of revenue data, which precludes more granular revenue-multiple approaches. Investors should treat this as a stage-appropriate anchor pending more complete operational disclosure.
